calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-13-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of The Court Record [Cal. R. Ct. 2.551(B)]
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CAMLO LOOPER's Motion To Seal Portions Of The Court Record [Cal. R. Ct. 2.551(B)].

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to seal portions of the court record in Waite v. Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Case No. CGC-24-613804, is granted. That case was filed but never served and was subsequently dismissed. Moving party Camlo Looper seeks an order that page 3, lines 6-8 and 18-24 ("Redacted Portions"), be redacted and removed from the public record. His motion has been served on the plaintiff in that case, Gary Waite.

Pursuant to Rule of Court 2.550 and 2.551, the court grants the requested relief. The Redacted Portions contain salacious material that could have been stricken on motion and that, since the case was dismissed by the plaintiff before Looper or his employer could defend it on the merits, can no longer be addressed or rebutted through the judicial process. The court finds under the unusual circumstances here that there is exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the Redacted Portions, to wit Looper's interest in his good name and public presentation; that the overriding interest supports sealing the Redacted Portions; that a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and that no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

The clerk of this court is directed to place the complaint in Waite v. Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Case No. CGC-24-613804, under seal, and to substitute in its place the redacted version attached as Exhibit A to the Fenske Declaration.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
